source_pr.title: domain, executor: fix sysvar cache race
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/25272 close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/25781

Problem Summary:

This fixes a race condition in the sysvar cache whilch could lead to lost updates with sysvar changes.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

A bug in the sysvar cache identified by flaky test cases has been fixed.

The fix includes using a separate mutex to protect against concurrent rebuilding of the sysvar cache. I originally considered using the same `svc.Lock()`, but this would likely lead to performance regressions since calls to `GetGlobalVar()` might block waiting for longer.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
~Unfortunately this could lead to performance regressions because `GetGlobalVar()` checks if the cache needs rebuilding (`rebuildCacheIfNeeded()`). This requires a read-lock, which will block waiting for longer because the lock is now held while reading the data. If this becomes observable, we may need to change the design to use 2 locks: one to protect the structures svc.session / svc.global and one to protect against rebuilding the cache.~

**This has been fixed.** I added a new mutex to protect against concurrent rebuild instead.

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
A race condition could occur in the sysvar cache that led to lost updates with the statement 'SET GLOBAL'.
```
